<user_query>
You are a dedicated reviewer. Inspect file humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py and related inheritance/config references. Focus on objective completeness, inheritance consistency, hidden assumptions, and risks of changing keys including probability-simplex handling, task-vs-AMP reward blend, and disabled stability rewards. Return prioritized findings with concrete file:line references and concrete suggested mitigations.
</user_query>

<git_status>

<agent_transcripts>
Agent transcripts (past chats) live in /Users/HanHu/.cursor/projects/Users-HanHu-software-motion-rl/agent-transcripts. They have names like <uuid>.jsonl, cite them to the user as [<title for chat <=6 words>](<uuid excluding .jsonl>). NEVER cite subagent transcripts/IDs; you can only cite parent uuids. Don't discuss the folder structure.
</agent_transcripts>

<agent_skills>
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge. To use a skill, read the skill file at the provided absolute path using the ReadFile tool, then follow the instructions within. When a skill is relevant, read and follow it IMMEDIATELY as your first action. NEVER just announce or mention a skill without actually reading and following it. Only use skills listed below.

<available_skills description="Skills the agent can use. Use the ReadFile tool with the provided absolute path to fetch full contents.">
<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md">Derive and checkout huh8-prefixed git branches while preserving already-prefixed branches, and prompt for explicit suffix when creating a new branch from a huh8 source branch. Use when the user asks to create, rename, or checkout branches with huh8 naming or company-ID branch conventions.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/commit-tag-prefix/SKILL.md">Prepends git commit messages with an angle-bracketed type tag and enforces a consistent format. Use when creating commit messages, preparing git commit text, or when the user asks to commit staged changes.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/deploy-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic Fuyao deployment flow with strict validation, branch sync, and confirmation.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/hp-sweep-orchestrator/SKILL.md">Orchestrates hyperparameter sweep training jobs on Fuyao. Use when the user mentions hyperparameter sweep, parameter search, training sweep, sweep jobs, or wants to deploy multiple training runs with different config values.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/motion-rl-isaacgym-exec/SKILL.md">Executes shell commands inside the isaacgym container for the Motion RL workspace. Use when the user mentions Motion RL, motion_rl, isaacgym-motion_rl workspace, or asks to run commands in that workspace.</agent_skill>

Use when the user mentions PlotJuggler, layout files, streaming config, UDP telemetry, fuzzy signal names, or wants fuzzy-to-exact signal mapping.</agent_skill>

Parses natural-language requests, gathers missing context through guided questions, and calls the tracker CLI to record nodes, export mermaid diagrams, and populate lineage tables. Use when the user mentions policy lineage, experiment tracking, mutations, runs, tasks, tracker nodes, exporting lineage graphs, or describes training results.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/reward-design-analyzer/SKILL.md">Analyzes RL reward design for a registered task by tracing config inheritance, categorizing all reward terms, ranking importance, identifying external disturbances, and producing a Markdown report with tuning suggestions. Use when the user asks to analyze rewards, understand reward design, trace reward configs, tune rewards, or wants a reward breakdown for a task in the motion_rl / humanoid-gym codebase.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sweep-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic orchestration of Fuyao hyperparameter sweeps with explicit preview and verification.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sync-after-skill-create/SKILL.md">Legacy fallback for toolbox sync guidance. Prefer the personal rule `~/.cursor/rules/sync-toolbox-after-toolbox-edits.mdc` for primary behavior when editing toolbox assets.</agent_skill>

Use when you want to create a rule, add coding standards, set up project conventions, configure file-specific patterns, create RULE.md files, or asks about .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-skill/SKILL.md">Guides users through creating effective Agent Skills for Cursor. Use when you want to create, write, or author a new skill, or asks about skill structure, best practices, or SKILL.md format.</agent_skill>

Use when you want to change editor settings, preferences, configuration, themes, font size, tab size, format on save, auto save, keybindings, or any settings.json values.</agent_skill>
</available_skills>
</agent_skills>

1. High — command-sampling intent in `stability_priority` is likely ineffective because `amp_scene_tag_ratio` has precedence over `commands.new_sample_methods`.

In `_resample_commands`, if `cfg.env` has `amp_scene_tag_ratio`, that branch is used and the `new_sample_methods` probabilities are ignored. The parent full-scenes config defines a 4-entry scene-tag ratio, while the child overrides only `new_sample_methods`, so the 0.80 stand bias in that file is not the active sampler.

```68:89:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py
class commands(R01V12SAAMPWith4DoFArmsAndHeadFullScenesCfg.commands):
    class new_sample_methods(R01V12SAAMPWith4DoFArmsAndHeadFullScenesCfg.commands.new_sample_methods):
        straight_prob = 0.09
        backward_prob = 0.04
        stand_prob = 0.80
        turn_prob = 0.07
```

```71:73:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes.py
amp_add_scene_tags = True
amp_scene_tag_lists = ["stand", "straight", "turn", "back"]
amp_scene_tag_ratio = [0.1, 0.4, 0.2, 0.3]
```

```164:203:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py
if not hasattr(self.cfg.env, 'amp_scene_tag_ratio'):
    return super()._resample_commands(env_ids)
...
if hasattr(self.cfg.env, 'amp_scene_tag_ratio'):
    prob_sum = 0.0
    for tag_name, tag_prob in zip(self.cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_lists, self.cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_ratio):
        ...
```

Mitigation: make only one sampling authority active per config. Either set `amp_scene_tag_ratio` in the same stability-priority class (or via override) to match your desired distribution, or explicitly disable scene-tag sampling path so `new_sample_methods` is used.

2. High — setting `amp_task_reward_lerp = 1.0` makes this config effectively task-only and disables discriminator shaping.

With `amp_task_reward_lerp` at 1.0, the blended reward becomes `disc_r * 0 + task_r * 1` by design. This is a hard semantic switch that may be easy to miss because other AMP-related config knobs remain set.

```161:163:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py
# What: Blend factor between AMP reward and task reward.
amp_task_reward_lerp = 1.0
```

```368:369:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_config.py
amp_task_reward_lerp = 0.3  # total reward = (1-param)*amp_reward + param*tasks_reward
```

```94:97:humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_discriminator.py
task_reward_lerp = self._get_task_reward_lerp(curriculum_iter)
r = (1.0 - task_reward_lerp) * disc_r + task_reward_lerp * task_r
```

Mitigation: if AMP behavior is still desired, reduce this to a task-AMP mix (e.g., 0.6–0.9) or use a stage-based curriculum (dict form) so the blend evolves over time.

3. Medium-High — no explicit simplex / sanity checks for sampling probabilities in either path.

Both branches compute interval boundaries from provided probabilities and then perform interval membership checks on a `[0,1)` random variable without normalization, range checks, or length consistency checks. This can silently change behavior (dead zones, overlaps, dropped probability mass, unintended bias) if values drift.

```173:189:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py
if hasattr(self.cfg.env, 'amp_scene_tag_ratio'):
    prob_sum = 0.0
    for tag_name, tag_prob in zip(self.cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_lists, self.cfg.env.amp_scene_tag_ratio):
        if tag_name == "straight":
            straight_prob_range = [prob_sum, prob_sum + tag_prob]
            prob_sum += tag_prob
...
```

```191:203:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py
    backward_prob_range = [
        straight_prob_range[1],
        straight_prob_range[1] + self.cfg.commands.new_sample_methods.backward_prob,
    ]
    stand_prob_range = [
        backward_prob_range[1],
        backward_prob_range[1] + self.cfg.commands.new_sample_methods.stand_prob,
    ]
    turn_prob_range = [
        stand_prob_range[1],
        stand_prob_range[1] + self.cfg.commands.new_sample_methods.turn_prob,
    ]
```

Mitigation: add defensive checks (sum close to 1.0, all probs >=0, tag-ratio length match) and a normalization or explicit error path when invalid.

4. Medium — `leg_only_control` + randomizing non-controlled joints increases unactuated-state variability without matching control authority.

`leg_only_control=True` zeroes torques beyond `leg_joint_dim`, while `randomize_non_controlled_joint_pos=True` injects variation into the same uncontrolled joints at reset. This can increase recovery burden and destabilize episodes, especially with push disturbances also enabled in the same class.

```46:50:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py
class env(...):
    leg_only_control = True
```

```453:455:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py
if getattr(self.cfg.env, "leg_only_control", False) and self.leg_joint_dim < self.num_dof:
    torques[:, self.leg_joint_dim :] = 0.0
```

```601:612:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py
def _randomize_non_controlled_joint_pos(self, env_ids):
    if not getattr(self.cfg.domain_rand, "randomize_non_controlled_joint_pos", False): return
...
    margin = float(getattr(self.cfg.domain_rand, "non_controlled_joint_pos_margin", 0.1))
```

Mitigation: tighten non-controlled joint randomization margin and ranges first in this mode, then re-check early-episode failures; if needed, keep a nonzero torso/pose prior in reward during early tuning.

5. Medium — explicit zero scales make several potential stability terms inert for this config.

The reward-builder removes all zero-scale entries before constructing reward functions, so any term left at 0 contributes nothing even if the corresponding function exists.

```107:127:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py
class scales(...):
    stand_still = 0.0
    orientation = 0.0
    torso_orientation = -2.0
    ang_vel_xy = 0.0
    lin_vel_z = 0.0
    base_height = 0.0
    default_joint_pos = 0.0
```

```934:940:humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py
for key in list(self.reward_scales.keys()):
    scale = self.reward_scales[key]
    if scale == 0:
        self.reward_scales.pop(key)
```

Mitigation: treat this as an explicit design decision and confirm it matches your "stability-priority" goal; if not, re-enable at least one of stand/pose/height or torso stability terms.

Open question to confirm before finalizing: do you want `new_sample_methods` or `amp_scene_tag_ratio` to be the intended control surface for this experiment family? If you want, I can provide a minimal patch plan with exact config edits for both branches plus sanity checks.
